Booking.title: Booking
Booking.description: Represents a booking for an aircraft.

pilotId.description: Reference to the Pilot making the booking.

instructorId.description: Reference to the Instructor for training sessions.

startTime.description: The start time of the booking.

endTime.description: The end time of the booking.

overnightId.description: A unique ID to link parts of an overnight booking.

cancellationReason.description: The reason for the booking cancellation.

PilotProfile.title: PilotProfile
PilotProfile.description: Represents a pilot's profile, including students, private pilots, and instructors.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PilotProfile entity.

contactNumber.description: The contact phone number of the pilot.

Personnel.title: Personnel
Personnel.description: Represents a non-flying staff member at the aviation academy.

id_.description: Unique identifier for the Personnel entity.

userType.description: The category of the user, fixed to 'Personnel'.

role.description: The ID of the role assigned to the personnel.

permissions.description: An array of permission IDs (strings) associated with this person, potentially overriding the role.

department.description: The ID of the department the personnel belongs to.

contactNumber.description: The contact phone number of the personnel.

issueDate.description: The date the license was issued.

ratings.description: List of ratings held by the pilot.

endorsements.description: List of endorsements held by the pilot.

Documents.title: Documents
Documents.description: A list of documents associated with a user or aircraft.

Aircraft.title: Aircraft
Aircraft.description: Represents an aircraft used for training.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Aircraft entity.

frameHours.description: Total hours on the aircraft frame.

tachoAtNext50Inspection.description: Tacho reading at which the next 50-hour inspection is due.

tachoAtNext100Inspection.description: Tacho reading at which the next 100-hour inspection is due.

maintenanceLogs.description: References to MaintenanceLog entries. (Relationship: Aircraft 1:N MaintenanceLog)

emptyWeight.description: The basic empty weight of the aircraft (in lbs).

emptyWeightMoment.description: The moment of the aircraft at its basic empty weight (in lbs-in).

stationArms.description: Defines the arm (distance from datum in inches) for each station.

baggage1.description: Arm for the primary baggage area.

baggage2.description: Arm for the secondary baggage area, if applicable.

cgEnvelope.description: An array of [weight, cg] pairs defining the vertices of the CG envelope polygon.

MaintenanceLog.title: MaintenanceLog
MaintenanceLog.description: Represents a maintenance log entry for an aircraft.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MaintenanceLog entity.

procedure.description: Details of the maintenance procedure followed.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SafetyProtocol entity.

description.description: Detailed description of the safety protocol.

complianceChecks.description: References to ComplianceCheck entries. (Relationship: SafetyProtocol 1:N ComplianceCheck)

id.description: Unique identifier for the ComplianceCheck entity.

date.description: Date when the compliance check was performed.

result.description: Result of the compliance check (true for pass, false for fail).

notes.description: Additional notes or observations from the compliance check.

Permission.title: Permission
Permission.description: Represents a permission within the application, typically generated from the menu structure.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Permission entity, often derived from the menu href.

name.description: The human-readable name of the permission (e.g., 'Users').

description.description: A description of what the permission grants.

Role.title: Role
Role.description: Represents a role within the application, defining a set of permissions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Role entity.

permissions.description: An array of permission IDs (strings) associated with this role.

requiredDocuments.description: A list of document names that a user with this role is required to upload.

Tenant.title: Tenant
Tenant.description: Represents a Tenant on the system.

Theme.title: Theme
Theme.description: Represents the application theme for a tenant.

Settings.title: Settings
Settings.description: Represents the application settings for a tenant.

tenantId.description: The tenant this setting belongs to.

WarningPeriod.title: WarningPeriod
WarningPeriod.description: Represents a single document expiry warning period with its associated color.

period.description: The number of days before expiration to send a warning.

color.description: The hex color code associated with this warning period.

DocumentExpiry.title: DocumentExpiry
DocumentExpiry.description: Represents the document expiry warning settings for a tenant.

defaultColor.description: The hex color code for documents not within any warning period.

expiredColor.description: The hex color code for documents that have expired.

warningPeriods.description: An array of warning period objects.

Department.title: Department
Department.description: Represents a department within a tenant.

currentNumber.description: The last number that was used.

ChecklistResponse.title: ChecklistResponse
ChecklistResponse.description: Represents a single submission of a pre-flight or post-flight checklist for a specific booking.

id.description: Unique identifier for the checklist response.

pilotId.description: Reference to the Pilot who submitted the checklist.

submissionTime.description: Timestamp of when the checklist was submitted.

responses.description: An array of responses for each checklist item.

ChecklistItemResponse.title: ChecklistItemResponse
ChecklistItemResponse.description: Represents a response to a single item within a checklist.

itemId.description: Identifier for the checklist item (e.g., 'doc_cert_of_reg' or 'photo_left_wing').

checked.description: Indicates if the item was checked/confirmed by the pilot.

notes.description: Any notes or comments provided by the pilot for this item.

photoUrl.description: URL of the uploaded photo for this item, if applicable.

tacho.description: The tacho reading for this item.

hobbs.description: The Hobbs meter reading for this item.

definition.description: Stores tenant information. All other collections except users are tenant specific.
Stores non-flying staff information, scoped to a specific tenant.
Stores pilot profile information (students, instructors, private pilots), scoped to a specific tenant.

params.name: pilotId
params.description: Unique identifier for the pilot profile.

definition.description: Stores aircraft information, scoped to a specific tenant.
Stores maintenance log entries for a specific aircraft, scoped to a specific tenant.

params.name: maintenanceLogId
params.description: Unique identifier for the maintenance log entry.

definition.description: Stores booking information, scoped to a specific tenant.
Stores safety protocols, scoped to a specific tenant.

params.name: safetyProtocolId
params.description: Unique identifier for the safety protocol.

definition.description: Stores compliance checks for a specific safety protocol, scoped to a specific tenant.

params.name: safetyProtocolId
params.description: Unique identifier for the safety protocol.

params.name: complianceCheckId
params.description: Unique identifier for the compliance check.

definition.description: Stores roles, scoped to a specific tenant.
Stores all available application permissions, scoped to a specific tenant.
Stores the application settings for a tenant. A single document can hold multiple settings, e.g., 'document-expiry'.

params.name: settingsId
params.description: Unique identifier for the settings document (e.g., 'theme', 'document-expiry').

definition.description: Stores departments, scoped to a specific tenant.
Stores distributed counters for generating sequential IDs.
Stores submitted checklist responses for bookings.

params.name: responseId
params.description: Unique identifier for the checklist response.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support a multi-tenant aviation academy management application, focusing on security, scalability, and ease of debugging. Authorization Independence is achieved through denormalization where needed, especially for collaborative data. Structural Segregation ensures that each collection has a homogeneous security posture. Access Modeling follows consistent patterns, utilizing path-based ownership for user-specific data and membership maps for collaborative entities. The structure is multi-tenant, where tenants are isolated from each other. The structure uses the tenants collection to keep a list of tenants and provides a tenant id for all other collections in the database. This strategy allows the application to manage multiple academies within the same system, each with its own set of data and settings.

*   **Tenants:** The root level collection `tenants` stores individual tenant documents. Each tenant has its own unique ID and associated settings.
*   **Users:** User data is now split into `personnel` (non-flying staff) and `pilots` (students, instructors, private pilots) under each tenant to better segregate data models and security rules.
*   **Settings:** A generic `settings` collection is added under each tenant. This allows for storing various configuration documents, such as `document-expiry`, in a structured and scalable way without creating new top-level collections for each setting type.
*   **ChecklistResponses:** A new collection to store the completed checklists, linked to a tenant.

QAPs (Rules are not Filters) is addressed by:

*   Segregating data into collections with homogeneous security requirements.
*   Using path-based ownership for private data.
*   Employing membership maps for collaborative data, and storing these maps directly within the documents to avoid `get()` calls in security rules.

Specifically, the following denormalization strategies are used:

*   Authorization context (e.g., members, ownerId) is copied into subcollection documents to avoid hierarchical dependencies.
*   Tenant ID is included in all documents to support multi-tenancy access control.

This design enables robust security rules that are simple to write, easy to understand, and highly performant.
